what_it_does: A live integration test that exercises a "self-growth" workflow: an LLM proposes a new node capability, the proposal is surfaced for operator approval, path-traversal names and self-approval are rejected, an operator approves through a separate channel, the node is written to a temporary nodes directory and auto-discovered, and a graph is executed to verify the new node works.
observations: Requires a reachable Ollama instance and skips if unavailable
Creates a temporary git repository so self-apply can commit
Uses a temporary nodes directory to avoid mutating the real repo
Tests path-traversal rejection before any model call or write
Tests that apply_node is blocked until operator approval
Calls suite.resolve_surfaced(prop['id'], 'approve') to simulate operator approval
Verifies the new 'reverse' node is discovered and produces 'olleh' from 'hello'
standards: End-to-end test of a system writing and adopting its own code under governance
Explicit operator-approval channel separate from the agent's path
conventions: Module-level docstring explains purpose, governance story, and run command
Uses a global 'ok' flag and a small 'check' helper for assertions
Inserts parent directory into sys.path for imports
Uses tempfile for isolated stores and node directories
concerns: Relies on a live model, making the test non-deterministic and environment-dependent
No cleanup of temporary directories or repositories
Subprocess git calls assume git is installed and do not handle failures explicitly
Generated code is executed without visible sandboxing beyond a temp directory
notes: A live, sandboxed proof that the system can grow its own node library only after an operator approves the proposal.
questions: What exactly does Suite.propose_node do and how is the LLM prompt constructed?
How is the operator approval channel secured from the agent in production?
What does resolve_surfaced change in the underlying governance state?
How does apply_node generate and commit the file?
What safety checks exist on the generated code before execution?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it demonstrates propose, governance approval, apply, discovery, and execution of a self-written capability.
intention_signals: Show that the system can extend itself by generating a new node from a request, but only under operator oversight, and then prove the new capability runs correctly.
novelty: A test that drives an LLM to author a new runtime node and verifies the full lifecycle from proposal through governance approval to execution.
contribution: A concrete, runnable scenario that encodes the self-growth governance contract and provides a regression target for proposal, approval, apply, discovery, and execution behavior.
summary_for_embedding: Live integration test that prompts an LLM to propose a new node, requires operator approval, writes it to a temporary node directory, auto-discovers it, and executes it in a graph.
intention_for_embedding: Verify governed self-growth: the system can generate and adopt a new capability only after operator approval, then run it correctly.

symbols.does: Updates a global pass/fail flag and prints a PASS/FAIL line for a labeled condition.
symbols.description: A lightweight assertion helper used throughout the test. It combines the result into a module-level 'ok' boolean and emits a readable status line, making the test self-reporting.

symbols.does: Orchestrates the live self-growth test: checks Ollama reachability, sets up temp repo and nodes dir, tests path-traversal rejection, proposes a node, blocks self-approval, simulates operator approval, applies the node, and runs a graph to verify it.
symbols.description: The main entry point for the integration test. It wires together NodeRegistry, Suite, governance, and scheduler to exercise the full propose-approve-apply-discover-run lifecycle, returning 0 only if every check passes.
